snippet.title: Mike Hsu Talks Sopranos With Steve "Bobby Baccalieri" Schirripa
snippet.description: Steve Schirripa play the sweet but deadly Bobby Baccalierie on "The Sopranos".  Some call The Sopranos the greatest television show of all time. It's been 16 years since it's last episode and people still love to discuss and analyze it.  You will get a chance to do that with Steve, and fellow cast members Michael Imperioli and Vincent Pastore on their Talking Sopranos tour at The Scubert Theater in Boston on March 11 and at The Strand in Providence on April 1. Mike Hsu talks to Schirripa about why people are still obsessed with the show, some misconceptions about the finale, and why Steve Perry from Journey is a liar.

snippet.title: Mike Hsu Talks To Kelly Hansen From Foreigner About Their Farewell Tour
snippet.description:
Foreigner are one of those bands who's music you'll be hearing on the radio, and backyard cook outs, supermarkets, and doctor's offices forever.  They have 16 Top 30 hits:
I Want to Know What Love Is (1)
Waiting for A Girl Like You (2)
Double Vision (2)
Hot Blooded (3)
Urgent (4)
Feels Like the First Time (4)
I Don’t Want to Live Without You (5)
Say You Will (6)
Cold as Ice (6)
That Was Yesterday (12)
Dirty White Boy (12)
Head Games (14)
Blue Morning, Blue Day (15)
Long, Long Way from Home (20)
Juke Box Hero (26)
Break It Up (26)

And I'm sure you know all the words to at least 5 of them.  But all good things must end and they are currently on their farewell tour.  Mike Hsu talks to singer Kelly Hansen on why they're calling it a career, if founder Mick Jones will be appearing at any shows, and why bassist Jeff Pillson is seated for the current shows.

snippet.description:
An interview with Mike Brown, Chief Legal Officer at Dexcom by Richard Hsu (@HsuUntied). More at https://hsuuntied.com/mbrown

In this candid conversation, Mike shares his journey from a reluctant law student avoiding pre-med to becoming the Chief Legal Officer at Dexcom, discussing his back-and-forth transitions between private practice and in-house roles, and how his deep commitment to mentoring shaped his entire career.

snippet.title: Mike Hsu Talks To Cliff Goodwin About recording His New Album At Abbey Road Studios
snippet.description: Worcester's own Cliff Goodwin started his first band the day after seeing The Beatles on their historic Ed Sullivan Show appearance in 1964.  So it is an understatement to say he was thrilled to be recording his new album, "Double It Up",  at Abbey Road Studios where they created all of their legendary music.  Cliff also spent 12 years playing with Joe Cocker, played on the Robert Palmer album Secrets as well as session work for many others.

snippet.title: The OCC’s Michael Hsu on the Big Risks Facing Banking Businesses Right Now | Odd Lots
snippet.description:
Earlier this year, we saw the collapse of Silicon Valley Bank and Silvergate Bank following a run on deposits and big losses on their portfolios of bonds. Since then, regulators have been discussing changes to existing bank regulation, prompting existential questions about the future of the US lending landscape. But there are other risks lurking in the banking system too — including those created by new technology and changing business models. In this episode, recorded live at Money 20/20 in Las Vegas, we speak with Michael Hsu, the acting comptroller of the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. He talks about banking regulation, crypto contagion, the rise of banking-as-a-service (BaaS) and the supply chain of payments.

Intro -- 00:00
The different US banking regulators — 5:05
The OCC’s view of stablecoins — 9:02
Crypto contagion to banks — 11:35
OCC interest in tokenization — 12:59
Guidance on third-party relationships — 18:05
The risks of third-party vendors — 22:21
The ideal structure of the US banking system— 27:46
Merger guidance post-SVB — 29:36
Could we have a Bank of Apple? — 30:39

He talks about banking regulation, crypto contagion, the rise of banking-as-a-service (BaaS) and the supply chain of payments.

snippet.title: Stephanie Hsu on Her Favorite Dual Roles, Being a Theater Geek & Protecting Her Art | Vanity Fair
snippet.description:
In this "A List" discussion, Stephanie Hsu sits down with Franklin Leonard to talk about her favorite dual role performances of all time, relative to her iconic dual role as Jobu Tupaki in the Oscar-winning film, "Everything Everywhere All At Once." From Mike Myers in the "Austin Powers" franchise to Donald Glover as Teddy Perkins in "Atlanta," the actress breaks down what those characters meant to her and her interpretations of their work. Stephanie also opens up about her childhood, journey to becoming an "experimental theater geek," and what she hopes to see for her future in Hollywood. 

Joy Ride is now in theaters.

snippet.title: Mike Hsu Talks To Music Journalist Jim Sullivan About His New Book Back Stage And Beyond Volume 1
snippet.description: Jim Sullivan has been writing about music for almost 50 years. He's collected some of his interview experiences in his new book "Backstage And Beyond Vol. 1: 45 Years Of Classic Rock Chats And Rants.  The book contains interviews and stories with Joe Perry from Aerosmith, Lou Reed, Neil Young, Tina Turner, Lemmy from Motorhead, Ray Davies, Jerry Lee Lewis and many others.  Jim talks to Mike Hsu about what it takes to get a subject to open up, what his most difficult interview was, and what's going to be in Volume 2.

snippet.title: Mike Hsu Talks To Comedian Aaron Weber About Nashville, Baseball And His Sweet Myspace Page

Mike Hsu talks to him about the Nashville comedy scene, visiting Fenway Park and Boston for the first time, and his sweet ass Myspace page.

snippet.title: Dale Arnold Talks To Mike Hsu About Hockey Enforcers And His New Book "The Tough Guys"
snippet.description: Dale Arnold is a New England broadcasting legend.  He has covered every New England sports team over his career but mostly The Boston Bruins.  With his vast knowledge and the help of some Hockey Enforcer icons he's written a book that documents a slowly disappearing role in pro-hockey: The Enforcer. He talks to Mike Hsu about how the game has changed and the great stories relayed to him by the "Tough Guys" themselves.
